% import-problem.tex -- a STANDALONE problem file for \importproblem, which
% \inputs it inline (no \begin{problem} wrapper -- the content renders directly).
% Its \setvar/\setrng are authored to DIFFER from the caller's overrides so a
% rendered value proves the override actually locked them (pbank_apply_fix +
% fixed[] guards).  \setvar{leak} is deliberately NOT overridden, so the caller
% can also confirm push_scope/pop_scope kept it from leaking out of the import.
\setvar{a}{77}%       a fixed override must beat this (never renders 77)
\setrng{b}{20}{29}%   a fixed override must beat this (fixed value is out of range)
\setvar{leak}{42}%    not overridden -> must stay scoped to the import
IMPSTART \get{a} and \get{b} and \get{c} IMPEND
